event_blocks.summary: The Male Performer in Dark Jacket (standing) implores the motionless Male Performer in Dark Jacket (lying), identified as Grimsby, to believe him about a real entity, but Grimsby dismisses it as an old tale, comparing it to sirens and mermaids. As the standing performer helps Grimsby sit up amidst dim, wave-like blue and green stage lighting, the Red-Haired Performer, adorned in a silver sequined top, green tail, and a white flower, enters from stage left. Swelling orchestral music accompanies her as she kneels by the Bust of a Man, holding a golden trident-like object, and performs a ritualistic ballad, gesturing towards the bust and Grimsby, singing, "I don't know when, I don't know how, but I know something's starting right now. Watch and you'll see. Someday I'll be part of you." Grimsby's eyes flutter open as she exits stage left. The stage darkens, with only faint blue light on the backdrop, and the music shifts to a whimsical orchestral theme. Ensemble Performers, including the Yellow-Striped Performer and Seagull Performer, emerge from stage right, dancing on a ship's deck-like platform under blue stage lighting, with a banner bearing an emblem behind them. The atmosphere becomes darker and more mysterious with further dimming of stage lights, leaving only faint blue projections on the backdrop while audience silhouettes are visible.

event_blocks.summary: On a dimly lit stage, bathed in vertical beams of deep blue and faint green light, the Mermaid Ensemble, comprising six performers in vibrant, sequined gold, red, orange, blue, and purple fish-tail costumes adorned with flowing fabric and seashell headdresses, commenced a synchronized dance. The backdrop featured a large, textured rock or coral formation, with subtle lighting shifts highlighting the ensemble as they maintained their semi-circle formation, occasionally breaking into smaller groupings. The performers continued to sing about Ariel being 'dizzy and dreamy,' with her 'head up in the foam' and 'eyes gone all gleamy,' floating away 'to daze' and giggling 'like a fool,' questioning if she's 'ill or insane' or has 'water on the brain.' The song built to a comedic crescendo with the exclamations, 'It's the bends! It's the flu! Gosh, I wish we had a clue. Oh wait, oh dear, good grief, it's clear she's...' event_blocks.summary: On a stage designed to resemble an underwater environment with a textured backdrop and deep blue, green, and faint yellow Stage Lighting, a Mermaid Ensemble of six performers, clad in colorful, sequined fish-tail costumes with distinct hair colors and seashell headdresses, executes a synchronized dance with flowing arm movements and coordinated steps. They sing lyrics such as 'She's in love, she's in love,' with one performer noting, 'Look, I think she's even wearing brand new shells,' and another exclaiming, 'Glory be, Lord above, got to be she's in love.' A large, gray, sculpted Bust of a Man adorned with colorful pom-poms stands as a central focal point, flanked by large Stage Props resembling rock/coral formations, later revealed to be stylized pink and purple Coral and Mushroom Props. The Yellow-Striped Performer, in a bright yellow and blue striped costume with spiky yellow hair, enters and joins the ensemble, making animated gestures. This performer exclaims, 'Hey Flounder!' and inquires about 'you know who,' prompting a female voice to respond, 'You mean Ariel? I'll say! She acts like she don't see me, she doesn't even speak. She treats me like sushi, leftover from last week.' The female voice continues, describing Ariel's late-night restlessness, 'You see her late at night, tossing in her ocean, then she shoots...' with the chorus echoing 'Shoot, shoot, shoot,' as she describes Ariel shooting at the harbor, rocks, and Tapper, concluding, 'She lays there like a lox.' The song also describes a mermaid who 'sings and she swims' and 'even has a sort of glow,' asking 'What on earth could it be?' before concluding 'Any hammerhead can see that glow,' and noting 'her hips, how they swish,' with the chorus questioning 'don't you wonder who's the lucky seafood dish?' The performers' fluid, aquatic motions, particularly the Yellow-Striped Performer's central role with dramatic gestures, illustrate the lyrics, while Audience Silhouettes are faintly visible along the bottom edge, indicating a live performance.

Mermaid Ensemble.description: A group of six performers dressed as mermaids in colorful, sequined fish-tail costumes with various hair colors (blue, pink, orange, red, gold, purple), some adorned with seashell headdresses, performing a synchronized dance with flowing arm movements and coordinated steps on a stage with a rock/coral backdrop, embodying the 'she' of the lively song who 'sings and she swims,' 'even has a sort of glow,' and whose 'hips... swish' as she is 'in love,' with the chorus asking 'don't you wonder who's the lucky seafood dish?'

Coral and Mushroom Props.description: Large, stylized props resembling coral and oversized mushrooms in pink and purple hues, placed on stage to enhance the fantastical underwater environment.

raw_segments: The video opens with a vibrant stage performance featuring a Mermaid Ensemble of six performers in colorful, sequined fish-tail costumes, each with distinct hair colors (blue, pink, orange, red, gold, purple) and some adorned with seashell headdresses, performing a synchronized dance with flowing arm movements and coordinated steps to an upbeat, theatrical musical number. They sing 'She's in love, she's in love,' with one performer adding, 'Look, I think she's even wearing brand new shells,' and another singing, 'Glory be, Lord above, got to be she's in love.' The stage is designed to resemble an underwater environment, with a dark, textured backdrop illuminated by vertical streaks of deep blue and faint green Stage Lighting. A large, gray, sculpted Bust of a Man, adorned with colorful pom-poms, serves as a central focal point, flanked by large, covered Stage Props resembling rock/coral formations. Midway through, a Yellow-Striped Performer, dressed in a bright yellow and blue striped costume with spiky yellow hair resembling a stylized sea creature, enters from stage center and joins the ensemble, making animated gestures with amusement. A male voice, presumably this performer, exclaims 'Hey Flounder!' and asks about 'you know who,' to which a female voice replies, 'You mean Ariel? I'll say! She acts like she don't see me, she doesn't even speak. She treats me like sushi, leftover from last week.' The female voice continues to describe Ariel's behavior, 'You see her late at night, tossing in her ocean, then she shoots...' with the chorus echoing 'Shoot, shoot, shoot,' as she describes Ariel shooting at the harbor, rocks, and Tapper, 'She lays there like a lox.' The performers’ movements and costumes contribute to the fantastical and immersive underwater theme, with the Audience Silhouettes faintly visible along the bottom edge of the frame, indicating a live performance.

The video opens on a stage bathed in deep blue and purple lighting, creating an underwater ambiance with a textured backdrop resembling rock formations. The Mermaid Ensemble, comprising six performers in colorful, sequined fish-tail costumes with vibrant hair and seashell headdresses, performs a synchronized dance. Center stage, the Yellow-Striped Performer, in a bright yellow and blue striped costume with spiky yellow hair, leads the group with animated gestures and expressive movements. As they sway their arms and flowing skirts in a choreographed routine mimicking swimming, a lively, theatrical song with a female lead vocalist and chorus plays. The lyrics describe a mermaid who 'sings and she swims' and 'even has a sort of glow,' asking 'What on earth could it be?' before concluding 'Any hammerhead can see that glow.' The song continues, describing her as 'in love' and noting 'her hips, how they swish,' with the chorus asking 'don't you wonder who's the lucky seafood dish?' The ensemble's fluid, aquatic motion perfectly illustrates these lyrics, especially as the Yellow-Striped Performer takes a central role, sometimes stepping forward to lead or gesture dramatically. The lighting shifts to include green and yellow spotlights, enhancing the dynamic visual interest. Large, stylized pink and purple Coral and Mushroom Props flank the stage, contributing to the fantastical underwater environment, while Audience Silhouettes observe the vibrant, theatrical performance along the bottom edge.

event_blocks.summary: On a dimly lit stage designed to resemble an underwater cavern, bathed in deep blue and purple lighting, a theatrical performance unfolds with a Yellow-Striped Performer, identified as Flounder, leading six Mermaid Ensemble performers in colorful, sequined fish-tail costumes. The mermaids, adorned with seashell headdresses, execute a synchronized, flowing dance with extended arms, while Flounder, in a bright yellow and blue striped costume with spiky hair, questions with a frantic male voice, "Where has she swum off to now? I got to find her for her daddy goes lookin'." A female voice from the Mermaid Ensemble playfully responds, "Good luck, she's in La La Land," adding that "That girl's head over heels in love." Flounder expresses surprise, "In love? Ariel?" and the mermaids press him, "Come on, Flounder, who's the lucky merman?" to which Flounder nervously replies, "Oh gee, I don't know. Didn't even know it was love until the second chorus." As the mermaids chant, "You follow her everywhere!", Flounder reveals, "Not anymore. Not since she started chasing after humans." This revelation elicits a surprised "Humans?" from the Red-Haired Performer, a character in a full-body red lobster costume with a top hat and googly eyes, who interacts dynamically with the group. Flounder repeats "Humans?" in shock, hoping he "didn't just say humans." As a mermaid teases "Uh-oh!", Flounder dreads the identity of Ariel's new love, while the Red-Haired Performer exasperatedly retorts, "Then don't ask me!" The mermaids express disbelief, "A real human? The four-legged kind? Dating outside of her species?", as the Red-Haired Performer worries, "Daddy's gonna be wet." The scene culminates in a chaotic chorus of "Wait, wait, wait!" from the performers, their panic palpable as the subtle spotlight shifts, enhancing the dramatic atmosphere. Large, covered props resembling rocks or coral formations flank the stage, contributing to the underwater illusion, while faint audience silhouettes observe the unfolding fantasy.

event_blocks.summary: In near darkness illuminated by faint blue and green stage lighting, a male performer in a white ruffled shirt, blue breeches, red sash, and black boots emerges center stage, his expression pensive and distressed as he questions, "What all around you, child, and you still got to play with fire." He walks towards two large, gray-draped props resembling rock or coral formations, pauses, and sits on one, leaning forward in exhaustion or deep thought as a gentle, melancholic orchestral melody with strings and woodwinds begins. Transitioning to song with a clear, expressive tenor voice, he laments, "Where did she go? Where can she be? When will she come again? Calling to me..." and describes a "girl who's like the shimmer of the wind upon the water" and "the glimmer of the sunlight on the sea," while the moody atmospheric lighting shifts to warm orange and dark blue hues, with faint audience silhouettes visible.

He slowly walks forward, then turns and climbs onto the covered prop, standing atop it as if surveying his surroundings, his song continuing. The atmosphere is somber and introspective. Suddenly, a new performer, the Male Performer in Tattered Coat, enters from stage left, dressed in a dark, tattered coat with a pink ruffled cravat and gray breeches, holding a small object. He approaches the central performer with an expressive, almost pleading gesture, interrupting the song to say, "Her voice... The newcomer then proposes, "I have a better idea. On the eve of your 21st birthday, we'll host a marvelous ball. The scene opens with two male performers on stage, accompanied by dramatic, slightly whimsical orchestral music. The Male Performer in Dark Jacket, dressed in a white ruffled shirt, blue knee-length breeches, a red sash, and black boots, stands with a pensive expression. Beside him, the Male Performer in Tattered Coat, in a dark, tattered coat, gray breeches, and a pink ruffled cravat, gestures expressively as if pleading or delivering a message, speaking in a theatrical voice about an "eligible young lady" and declaring, "I knew it! to sing for you." He then announces, "What? A contest! What better way to find your mysterious voice than that? You'll be married by sunset, on that very same day, just as your father decreed." The Male Performer in Dark Jacket responds with a simple "Yes." The Male Performer in Tattered Coat continues, "Well yes, that's how we'll find her. Indeed we shall. You have my word. She's out there somewhere. I just know it." He then moves toward the center before exiting stage left, leaving the Male Performer in Dark Jacket alone. The stage backdrop features a dark, textured design with warm orange and blue lighting, evoking an underwater or cavernous environment, enhanced by the Stage Lighting. The Male Performer in Dark Jacket walks slowly across the stage, pausing to look upward with a reflective or distressed expression. As the music transitions to a melancholic, searching ballad, he begins to sing, "I can sense her laughter in the ripple of the waves against the shoreline. I can see her smiling in the moonlight as it settles on the sand. I can feel her waiting just beyond the pale horizon, singing out a melody too lovely to withstand. And her voice is there..." He then approaches a covered prop resembling a rock or coral formation (Stage Props (Rock/Coral Formations)), where he sits down. He remains seated, appearing weary or contemplative, as the spotlight follows his movements, his singing continuing. The Audience Silhouettes are faintly visible at the bottom of the frame, indicating a live performance setting. The atmosphere is somber and introspective, with minimal props and focused lighting emphasizing the central character’s emotional state and his longing song.

event_blocks.summary: The scene opens in near-total darkness with a grand orchestral piece that transitions to a whimsical tune, accompanied by faint blue and green stage lighting creating ethereal patterns, with audience silhouettes visible. A single spotlight reveals a Mermaid Performer with long red hair and a shimmering green tail, entering from the right with fluid motions, shortly followed by a Red-Haired Performer in a whimsical red lobster costume from the left. They meet center stage, engaging in playful, expressive interaction. The Red-Haired Performer questions, "Child, there you are. A human? You fell in love with a human?" to which the Mermaid Performer defensively replies, "I didn't choose it, it just happened." The Red-Haired Performer insists, "Oh, child. Happy for you? Happy? 'Cause you're gone plumb crazy now. Child, it's time you get your head out of the clouds and back in the water where it belongs," and the Mermaid Performer protests, "But I don't belong here. I've never..." The Mermaid Performer expresses a longing to be "up there" on land, feeling she doesn't "fit in" and wishing she could walk on the same ground as "he is," breathing the same air. The Red-Haired Performer counters, "Down here is your home. The human world, it's a mess. Life under the sea is better than anything they got up there," launching into a lively musical number, "Believe me, child, it's true. The seaweed is always greener..." As the camera pulls back, the full stage is revealed under deep blue and green stage lighting with a rocky or coral-like backdrop. Ensemble Performers enter from both sides, dressed in colorful, period-style costumes, carrying translucent umbrellas in pastel hues, forming a line and performing synchronized, flowing movements. The ensemble grows, including a Yellow-Striped Performer who leads the group with animated gestures. The stage transforms into a vibrant, fantastical underwater realm, illuminated by dynamic blue and purple stage lighting, with stylized rock and coral formations, to the lively, orchestral sounds of 'Under the Sea,' featuring prominent brass, woodwinds, harp, bass, and percussion. The Mermaid Ensemble in sequined fish-tail costumes and Sea Creature Performers in animal-inspired headpieces perform synchronized, fluid movements. The Sea Witch Performer, in a dramatic dark gown, commands the stage with theatrical gestures, mirrored by Flanking Performers in matching dark, textured costumes. Translucent Umbrellas sway gently, adding an ethereal quality. The Red-Haired Performer dances energetically, interacting with the group, while the lyrics mention various sea creatures. The Mermaid Performer gracefully engages playfully with the Red-Haired Performer. The song emphasizes that 'life is the bubbles' under the sea, creating a cohesive and enchanting visual and auditory narrative.

The two central performers continue their interaction, then move toward the center as additional Ensemble Performers enter from both sides, dressed in colorful, period-style costumes with ruffled sleeves and skirts, carrying translucent umbrellas in pastel hues. They form a line across the stage, performing synchronized, flowing movements that resemble a dance to the ongoing musical number. The ensemble grows as more performers join, including a Yellow-Striped Performer in a bright yellow and blue striped costume with spiky yellow hair, who leads the group with animated gestures, participating in the choreographed musical number about how "under the sea" is better. The stage is flanked by large, covered props resembling rocks or coral, and the lighting shifts subtly to spotlight the central figures during the performance. The overall atmosphere is theatrical and fantastical, blending elements of a sea adventure with a vibrant musical performance, all observed by Audience Silhouettes along the bottom edge in near darkness.

The video opens with a wide shot of a vibrant stage performance set in an underwater-themed environment, bathed in dynamic blue and purple lighting that casts an ethereal glow over stylized rock and coral formations. An upbeat, calypso-style musical number, 'Under the Sea,' begins, with a male lead singer crooning about escaping the sun's toil to float 'under the sea.' A diverse ensemble of performers, including the Mermaid Ensemble, Sea Creature Performers, and the Red-Haired Performer in a red lobster costume with a top hat and googly eyes, are arranged across the stage, engaging in a synchronized, playful dance. As the chorus joins in, singing 'Down here all the fish is happy,' the performers move rhythmically with expressive gestures, their joy mirroring the song's celebration of underwater life. The Red-Haired Performer interacts animatedly with the Mermaid Performer, who has long red hair and a shimmering green tail, and the Yellow-Striped Performer, who wears a bright yellow and blue striped costume, leading the choreography with energetic movements, while several performers carry translucent pastel umbrellas, adding a whimsical, floating effect. The song continues with lyrics contrasting the happy fish with those on a line or in a bowl, building anticipation. As the camera zooms in, highlighting coordinated steps and joyful expressions, the iconic chorus erupts: 'Under the Sea, Under the Sea! Nobody beat us, fry us and eat us in fricassea!' The lighting subtly shifts, spotlighting key performers as the ensemble continues their fluid motions, occasionally forming lines or circles. The Red-Haired Performer gestures expressively, drawing attention, while the song emphasizes that 'life is the bubbles' under the sea, creating a cohesive and enchanting visual and auditory narrative of a lively, theatrical underwater world.

The stage transforms into a vibrant, fantastical underwater realm, illuminated by dynamic blue and purple stage lighting that casts shimmering beams across the performers, all set to the lively, orchestral sounds of a Broadway-style musical number, clearly 'Under the Sea'. A lead male vocalist sings about life "under the sea," joined by a chorus, as the music features prominent brass, woodwinds like a flute, harp, and bass, along with percussion, creating a celebratory atmosphere. The backdrop features stylized rock and coral formations, enhancing the aquatic illusion. A diverse ensemble, including the Mermaid Ensemble in colorful sequined fish-tail costumes, performs synchronized, fluid movements, embodying the joyous "under the sea" theme. The Sea Witch Performer, in a dramatic dark gown with purple embellishments, commands the stage with theatrical gestures, while Flanking Performers in matching dark, textured costumes mirror her movements. Translucent Umbrellas, held by several performers, sway gently, adding an ethereal, whimsical quality to the choreography. The Red-Haired Performer, now in a full-body red lobster costume with a top hat and large googly eyes, dances energetically, interacting with the group and gesturing expressively, while the lyrics mention various sea creatures like the sturgeon and the ray. The Mermaid Performer, with long red hair and a shimmering green tail, enters gracefully, engaging playfully with the Red-Haired Performer. Sea Creature Performers, including one with an orange fish headpiece and another with a purple shell headdress, flank the stage, contributing to the immersive underwater theme, as the song continues to describe musical sea creatures like the newt playing the flute, the carp playing the harp, and the bass playing the bass, punctuated by vocalizations like "Ay-ay-ay!" and "Whoo!". Coral and Mushroom Props in pink and purple hues enrich the fantastical environment. The lighting shifts subtly, spotlighting key performers and enhancing the mood, while Audience Silhouettes remain faintly visible at the bottom edge, observing the magical spectacle.

The video segment opens with a vibrant, underwater-themed stage performance, illuminated by dynamic blue, green, and purple lighting that creates an ethereal, aquatic atmosphere. The stage is adorned with large, stylized coral and mushroom props in pastel hues, enhancing the fantastical setting. A diverse ensemble of performers, including the Mermaid Ensemble in colorful sequined fish-tail costumes and Sea Creature Performers in animal-inspired headpieces, are positioned across the stage, moving energetically to the accompaniment of lively, upbeat, and theatrical music, reminiscent of a carnival or big-band sound, punctuated by enthusiastic audience cheers and applause. The central Sea Witch Performer, in a dramatic dark gown with a sheer skirt and purple embellishments, commands attention, flanked by two Flanking Performers in matching dark, textured costumes. They perform synchronized, theatrical gestures, including crouching and lunging, as the Sea Witch raises her arms expressively, with dramatic 'A-ya!' vocalizations heard, possibly from her or other performers, adding to the theatricality. event_blocks.summary: On a stage designed as a ship's deck, Ensemble Performers in colorful, period-style costumes with ruffled sleeves and skirts danced energetically, holding pastel Translucent Umbrellas under dynamic blue and green Stage Lighting that created an underwater atmosphere, accompanied by an upbeat, Caribbean-style musical number with male vocals celebrating life "Under the Sea" and mentioning sea creatures. Following applause and cheering from the Audience Silhouettes, the lighting shifted to spotlight the Red-Haired Performer, costumed as a lobster with a top hat and googly eyes, who emerged from the ensemble. As the group dispersed and the stage darkened with a dramatic musical underscore, a Mermaid Performer with long red hair, a shimmering green tail, and a seashell top entered from stage left, holding a trident, prompting Sebastian to anxiously call out "Ariel... Hello?". The Red-Haired Performer, speaking with an angry male voice, interrupted, demanding "Sebastian! Where is she?" and expressing fury that "Ariel" had "betray[ed] her family" and "turn[ed] her back on her own kind." Sebastian, addressing the Red-Haired Performer as "sire," attempted to defend Ariel, but was dismissed as the Red-Haired Performer declared she was "making a mockery of everything we hold dear" and questioned "Where's she hiding?" The stage remained dimly lit with deep blue tones and subtle sea-like projections as the two central performers faced each other.

Translucent Umbrellas.description: Pastel-colored, semi-transparent umbrellas carried by ensemble performers, adding a whimsical, ethereal quality to the choreography and stage presence.

Mermaid Performer.description: A performer with long red hair, wearing a shimmering green mermaid tail and a white top with seashell embellishments, entering the stage gracefully and interacting with the Red-Haired Performer in a playful, expressive manner, holding a trident, and identified by name as 'Ariel' in the dialogue.

Red-Haired Performer.description: A performer in a full-body red lobster costume with a top hat and large googly eyes, who emerges from the ensemble, gestures expressively, and later interacts with the Mermaid Performer in a theatrical, playful manner, speaking with a deep, authoritative, and angry male voice, identified as a 'sire,' expressing fury over Ariel's betrayal.

Ensemble Performers.description: A group of performers on a raised platform resembling a ship’s deck, dressed in colorful, period-style costumes with ruffled sleeves and skirts, performing a choreographed dance or musical number under blue stage lighting, holding Translucent Umbrellas in pastel hues, accompanied by an upbeat, Caribbean-style song with male vocals celebrating life 'Under the Sea' and mentioning various sea creatures.

Audience Silhouettes.description: Faint, dark outlines of seated individuals visible along the bottom edge of the frame, providing applause and cheering after the ensemble performance.

Stage Lighting.description: Vertical streaks of deep blue and faint green light projected onto a dark backdrop, creating a textured, atmospheric effect that shifts subtly to spotlight the central performer, enhancing the underwater illusion.

raw_segments: The video opens with a vibrant ensemble performance on a stage designed to resemble a ship’s deck, set against a dark, textured backdrop illuminated by dynamic blue and green stage lighting that evokes an underwater atmosphere. The Ensemble Performers, dressed in colorful, period-style costumes with ruffled sleeves and skirts, dance energetically while holding Translucent Umbrellas in pastel hues, creating a whimsical, ethereal effect. An upbeat, Caribbean-style musical number with male vocals plays, celebrating life "Under the Sea" and mentioning various sea creatures like clams, slugs, and snails, perfectly complementing the synchronized choreography. As the music fades, applause and cheering from the Audience Silhouettes fill the air. The lighting then shifts to spotlight a central figure—the Red-Haired Performer in a full-body red lobster costume with a top hat and large googly eyes—who emerges from the ensemble, gesturing expressively. The group disperses, leaving the stage darkened except for three spotlights focused on the Red-Haired Performer, as a new, more dramatic musical underscore begins. A Mermaid Performer with long red hair, wearing a shimmering green mermaid tail and white seashell top, enters from stage left, holding a trident. A male voice, identified as Sebastian, anxiously calls out "Ariel... Hello?", revealing the Mermaid Performer's name. The Red-Haired Performer, speaking with a deep, authoritative, and angry male voice, interrupts, demanding "Sebastian! Where is she?" and expressing his fury that "Ariel" has "betray[ed] her family" and "turn[ed] her back on her own kind." Sebastian attempts to defend her, calling the Red-Haired Performer "sire," but the Red-Haired Performer dismisses him, stating she's "making a mockery of everything we hold dear" and questioning "Where's she hiding?" The stage remains dimly lit with deep blue tones and subtle projections of sea-like textures, enhancing the aquatic theme, as the two central performers face each other, poised for their dramatic exchange.

event_blocks.summary: The dimly lit stage, bathed in deep blue and green hues, evokes an underwater ambiance as the King Triton Performer, with long silver hair, golden crown, and blue-gold lower garment, stands center stage holding a golden trident, facing the Red-Haired Performer, Sebastian, in a red lobster costume, who is told by Triton, "She keeps secrets from me, Sebastian, not from you. I'll wager you know where she is. Now take me to her." The stage lighting dramatically shifts to near darkness, revealing a cavernous backdrop with a glowing emblem as orchestral, dramatic music begins; the Male Performer in Dark Jacket, Eric, lies slumped against a gray bust, appearing unconscious, and Sebastian approaches, followed by the Seagull Performer who comforts Eric. The Mermaid Performer, Ariel, with red hair and a green tail, enters, interacting with Sebastian, and speaks with a soft, melancholic voice, "To think that this belonged to him once. He held it in his own two hands." King Triton reappears, addressing Eric, "Eric! Tell the truth. Did you save a human from drowning?" A high-pitched, frantic female voice interjects, "Who told? Which one of you?" followed by an apologetic, whiny female voice, "I'm sorry, Ariel! I'm nothing but a guppy. Just a lousy, lousy guppy. You get every single solo, and it isn't fair!" King Triton commands, "That's enough. Leave us alone." The Yellow-Striped Performer leads a Mermaid Ensemble in a synchronized dance, while Sebastian interacts with Ariel amidst Coral and Mushroom Props and Sea Creature Performers, with spotlights highlighting performers as Audience Silhouettes observe. Later, the Mermaid Performer, Ariel, with long red hair and a shimmering green mermaid tail, lies prone on the floor, appearing distressed, and the Seagull Performer leans over her, singing/speaking, "Never again! Take flight! Stop! Stop! Stay, no!" accompanied by a bell sound. The Red-Haired Performer, Sebastian, emerges from shadows, calling "Ariel." Ariel sits up, sobbing, "He ruined all of my things! My human stuff. If he really and truly loved me..." Sebastian interrupts, "Oh, child, it's because he loves ya." Ariel retorts, "That's sure a funny way of showing it," followed by a "boing" sound, and declares, "He can break every single treasure I own, but he can't break me!" Sebastian warns, "Well, you just might break his heart," prompting Ariel to accuse, "And you! Some friend you turn out to be! Bringing him..." The scene then shifts to the Mermaid Performer holding a large, gray sculpted bust of a man, singing, "to my secret place." A deep, resonant male voice warns, "For your own good, child. I pray you wake up from this madness. And soon you're swimming into dangerous waters." Ariel sings, "If only he would look, if only he could see, could love me as I am instead of how he wished I'd be," intensifying to "He doesn't really care, he doesn't want to know. I hate him, yes, I hate him, oh, I hate him so! And oh, what I would give, if only I could go!" Two Flanking Performers in dark, textured costumes enter, and the deep male voice comments, "Poor child, oh, sweet child. She is a very silly..." The Mermaid Performer, holding the bust, is approached by the Flanking Performers who sing, "if only... there was something... we could do," prompting Ariel to ask, "Who are you two?" They sing, "Sweet child... poor child... so treasured... so misunderstood... Dear child... sad child... Life's looking... shall we say... not good... no..." offering to "ease your woes and worries," asking, "Who'll help her forget her man? Sweet child... perhaps the sea which cares..." Ariel exclaims in shock, "You can't possibly mean Aunt Ursula?" The Flanking Performers confirm, singing, "She knows your true... everyone says her magic is evil," but add, "She'll grant your prayer." Ariel urgently states, "Please, I have to go!" as they persuade, "She'll cast a charm... a tiny spell... why be alone?" The moody, atmospheric lighting emphasizes the emotional weight of this interaction.

Mermaid Performer.description: A performer with long red hair, wearing a shimmering green mermaid tail and a white top with seashell embellishments, holding a large gray sculpted bust of a man. She displays sorrowful and contemplative expressions, reacting with subtle shifts in emotion and clutching her tail, while her distinct voice expresses initial melancholy as she sings, then surprise, shock, and fear as she questions the Flanking Performers and reacts to their suggestions.

Red-Haired Performer.description: A performer in a full-body red lobster costume with a top hat and large googly eyes, who briefly interacts with the Mermaid Performer through expressive gestures before exiting stage right, leaving her alone with the bust.

Bust of a Man.description: A large, gray, sculpted bust of a man with detailed facial features, held by the Mermaid Performer, serving as a focal point for the scene’s emotional and symbolic weight, particularly as the Flanking Performers refer to it in their song, asking 'Who'll help her forget her man?'

Yellow-Striped Performer.description: A performer in a bright yellow and blue striped costume with spiky yellow hair, resembling a stylized sea creature, who leads the ensemble with animated gestures and expressive movements, often stepping forward to command attention.

Mermaid Ensemble.description: A group of six performers dressed as mermaids in colorful, sequined fish-tail costumes with various hair colors (blue, pink, orange, red, gold, purple), some adorned with seashell headdresses, performing a synchronized dance with flowing arm movements and coordinated steps on a stage with a rock/coral backdrop.

Coral and Mushroom Props.description: Large, stylized props resembling coral and oversized mushrooms in pink and purple hues, placed on stage to enhance the fantastical underwater environment.

Male Performer in Dark Jacket.description: A male performer, identified as Eric, in a white ruffled shirt, blue pants, and black boots, initially lying slumped against the bust, appearing unconscious or resting, and later addressed by King Triton.

Seagull Performer.description: A performer in a large, comical seagull costume with a yellow beak, white feathery wings, and a gray body, leaning over the prone Mermaid Performer, placing a hand on her shoulder in a comforting or theatrical manner, who dramatically sings/speaks 'Never again! Take flight! Stop! Stop! Stay, no!' accompanied by a distinct bell or chime sound.

Audience Silhouettes.description: Faint, dark outlines of seated individuals visible along the bottom edge of the frame, likely audience members or performers in shadow, with no discernible features due to low lighting.

Sea Creature Performers.description: Performers in costumes resembling sea animals, including one with a large orange fish headpiece and another with a purple shell-like headdress, positioned on either side of the stage, adding to the underwater theme.

King Triton Performer.description: A central male performer with long silver hair, a golden crown, and a bare chest, wearing a blue and gold lower garment, holding a golden trident, engaging in dramatic gestures and dialogue with the Mermaid Performer, whose speech reveals his deep-seated hatred for humans, his grief over the death of the Mermaid's mother, his anger at the Mermaid's actions, and his paternal relationship with her, while also addressing an unseen character named 'Sebastian'.

Stage Lighting.description: Vertical streaks of deep blue and faint green light projected onto a dark backdrop, creating a textured, atmospheric effect that spotlights the central performers and enhances the underwater illusion.

Stage Props (Rock/Coral Formations).description: Two large, covered props resembling rocks or coral formations, draped in gray fabric, positioned on stage, serving as part of the scenic design and contributing to the underwater environment.

Flanking Performers.description: Two performers in matching dark, textured costumes with green and gold patterns, entering from stage right and moving slowly toward the center with synchronized, theatrical gestures. They surround the Mermaid Performer, engaging in expressive, ceremonial interaction while singing in harmonious, persuasive voices that offer sympathetic but manipulative advice, hint at magical solutions, and attempt to sway the Mermaid Performer with promises of easing her woes and granting her prayers.

The scene unfolds on a dimly lit stage designed as an underwater grotto, bathed in deep blue lighting with rock-like backdrops, creating an aquatic atmosphere. A dramatic and emotional exchange takes place between the Mermaid Performer, with long red hair and a shimmering green mermaid tail, and the King Triton Performer, a regal figure with long silver hair, a golden crown, and a golden trident. King Triton, addressing an unseen "Sebastian," initiates the heated discussion, gesturing expressively and raising his trident. The Mermaid Performer, holding a flowing green fabric, responds with animated hand movements, defending her actions by stating, "I had to rescue the prince." King Triton, his voice filled with anger, retorts, "You had to? Without me he would have perished. He's a human, you are a mermaid," emphasizing the stark divide. The Mermaid counters, "I can't hate him. Just because he's different. Perhaps you can, but it's just not in me," expressing her empathy. King Triton's rage escalates as he recounts human cruelty, "They catch us with their nets. They spear us with their blades. Have you forgotten what they did to your mother? She's left out one morning for an instant swim and never returns. I know in my heart, and that's enough. Murderers, all of them." The Mermaid attempts to reason, "You can't blame all humans for a few wicked ones." As dramatic, swelling orchestral music begins, King Triton, moving purposefully across the stage, vehemently declares, "I will not have you build a shrine to them. Worshiping their rubbish! To the prince that only they know!" The Mermaid cries out, "Daddy, no!" as King Triton shouts, "Never!" The stage lighting subtly spotlights their intense interaction, with a bust of a man, adorned with colorful pom-poms, and shelves with sea-themed objects serving as silent witnesses to this pivotal, emotionally charged confrontation, while Audience Silhouettes remain faintly visible at the bottom.

The scene unfolds on a dimly lit, underwater-themed stage, bathed in deep blue and green lighting that casts an ethereal glow, with a backdrop featuring a textured projection resembling a sea creature or coral. A Mermaid Performer with long red hair and a shimmering green tail lies prone on the floor, appearing distressed. A Seagull Performer, in a comical, oversized costume, leans over her, placing a comforting hand on her shoulder, dramatically singing/speaking, "Never again! Take flight! Stop! Stop! Stay, no!" accompanied by a distinct bell or chime sound. As the Seagull Performer steps back, a Red-Haired Performer in a full-body red lobster costume with a top hat emerges from the shadows, approaching the Mermaid with expressive, theatrical movements. The Red-Haired Performer softly, almost singing, calls out "Ariel." The Mermaid Performer slowly begins to sit up, clutching a piece of green fabric, sobbing, "He ruined all of my things! My human stuff. If he really and truly loved me..." The Red-Haired Performer gently interrupts, "Oh, child, it's because he loves ya." The Mermaid, slightly indignant, retorts, "That's sure a funny way of showing it," followed by a "boing" sound. With renewed determination, she declares, "He can break every single treasure I own, but he can't break me!" The Red-Haired Performer then warns, "Well, you just might break his heart," prompting the Mermaid to angrily accuse, "And you! Some friend you turn out to be! Bringing him..." The stage is flanked by two large, covered props resembling rocks or coral, enhancing the underwater illusion, while audience silhouettes remain faintly visible, observing the unfolding drama under the shifting spotlight.

The Mermaid Performer then remains alone, cradling the bust with a sorrowful and contemplative expression, her red hair flowing as she gazes at the object, occasionally lifting her head as if in prayer or mourning, while a clear, expressive female voice sings, "to my secret place." And soon you're swimming into dangerous waters." The deep male voice returns, commenting, "Poor child, oh, sweet child. She is a very silly..." The static stage props, including the covered rock/coral formations, and faint Audience Silhouettes enhance the quiet drama and somber, reflective mood, centered on the Mermaid Performer’s emotional connection to the bust.

A Mermaid Performer, with long red hair and a shimmering green mermaid tail, stands center stage, holding a large gray sculpted bust of a man, her expression sorrowful and contemplative as she sings, "serious problem, hasn't she?" Two Flanking Performers, dressed in dark, textured costumes with green and gold patterns, enter from stage right, approaching her with synchronized, theatrical gestures. They sing in harmonious, sympathetic tones, "if only... there was something... we could do," prompting the Mermaid Performer to question, "Who are you two?" Sweet child... perhaps the sea which cares..." The Mermaid Performer reacts with subtle shifts in emotion, clutching the green fabric of her tail, her gaze alternating between the bust and the performers, before she exclaims in shock, "You can't possibly mean Aunt Ursula?" The Flanking Performers acknowledge this, singing, "She knows your true... everyone says her magic is evil," but quickly add, "She'll grant your prayer." The moody, atmospheric lighting, shifting from deep blue to purple, emphasizes the emotional weight of this intense interaction, with the Audience Silhouettes faintly visible along the bottom edge.

event_blocks.summary: The scene opens with the Mermaid Performer, holding a large gray sculpted Bust of a Man and adorned in a shimmering green mermaid tail and white seashell top, standing center stage with two Flanking Performers in dark, textured costumes. Deep blue and purple Stage Lighting creates an underwater ambiance, with Stage Props resembling rock/coral formations flanking the scene. Multiple female voices sing harmoniously, with lyrics like "No one will tell, no one will care..." A deep, guttural growl punctuates the air as the Mermaid Performer, assisted by the Flanking Performers, carries the bust offstage left. Ominous synth music builds tension as a ghostly projection of a man’s face appears on the backdrop, prompting a distressed male voice to call out, "Hello? Ariel? It's me, Flounder. Where did you go?" The Mermaid Performer reappears, walking mournfully and looking at the projection, as the male voice continues, "Ursula! Oh, Ariel!" The Male Performer in Dark Jacket joins her, and a deeper male voice announces, "Mistress of the deep, you have a visitor!" The Male Performer sits, appearing weary, while the Mermaid Performer stands beside him, still holding the bust, as the scene ends in quiet contemplation. Later, the Sea Witch Performer, a theatrically menacing figure, enters and addresses the Mermaid Performer as "darling" and "Auntie Ursula," dismissing warnings about her father and stating, "We are very much alike, you and I. Gals with ambition? Nothing scares a man more, does it?" The Sea Witch proposes a spell to turn the Mermaid into a human for three days, with the condition of a kiss from the prince before sunset on the third day, or the Mermaid's soul will be forfeit. The Sea Witch, with spiky white hair and a voluminous dark gown, sings about helping "poor, unfortunate souls," while the Mermaid Performer, with long red hair and a shimmering green tail, holds the bust, her emotions shifting from sorrow to defiance. The Sea Witch then reveals her fee: the Mermaid's voice, which she claims will be stored in a "magic shell." The Mermaid Performer, hesitant, questions, "But if I give away my voice, how can I ever..." as the Flanking Performers move ritualistically. The Sea Witch, holding a glowing cauldron, urges the Mermaid to sign a scroll, declaring, "Got some jets and now I got a voice. The boss is on our own." The Mermaid, distressed by the prospect of never seeing her family again, ultimately agrees to the deal, exclaiming, "Yes! Yes, I'll do it! Where do I sign?" The Sea Witch instructs her to use her ink, and a magical "whoosh" sound and a gasp from the Mermaid Performer follow, as the Flanking Performers react with theatrical gestures, culminating in a moment of intense interaction between the Sea Witch and the Mermaid Performer.

Flanking Performers.description: Two performers in matching dark, textured costumes with green and gold patterns, entering from stage right and moving slowly toward the center with synchronized, theatrical gestures, adding a ritualistic or ceremonial presence to the scene, leaning in to observe the scroll and reacting to the interaction between the Sea Witch Performer and Mermaid Performer.

Stage Props (Rock/Coral Formations).description: Two large, covered props resembling rocks or coral formations, draped in gray fabric, positioned on stage, serving as part of the scenic design and contributing to the underwater environment, flanking the central performance area.

Male Performer in Dark Jacket (standing).description: A male performer with short dark hair, wearing a white ruffled shirt, blue knee-length breeches, a red sash, and black boots, who walks slowly across the stage with a pensive expression, eventually sitting on a covered prop, appearing weary or reflective.

Mermaid Performer.description: A performer with long red hair, wearing a shimmering green mermaid tail and a white top with seashell embellishments, holding a large scroll with visible handwritten text, displaying focused and distressed expressions as she interacts with the Sea Witch Performer and Flanking Performers. Her voice is initially pleading and distressed, expressing her fear of losing her family ("But if I become human, I'll never see my family again. My sisters, even my father"), before becoming desperate and resigned as she agrees, "Yes! Yes, I'll do it! Where do I sign?" A gasp/scream is heard from her after the Sea Witch's final instruction.

Bust of a Man.description: A large, gray, sculpted bust of a man with detailed facial features, held by the Mermaid Performer, serving as a focal point for the scene’s emotional and symbolic weight.

Stage Lighting.description: Vertical streaks of deep blue and faint green light projected onto a dark backdrop, creating a textured, atmospheric effect that spotlights the central performers and enhances the underwater illusion, with dynamic beams shifting to emphasize key moments.

Sea Witch Performer.description: A central performer with spiky white hair, dramatic makeup, and a voluminous dark gown featuring a sheer skirt and black corset-like bodice with purple embellishments, commanding the stage with expressive gestures and theatrical poses, holding a glowing cauldron and later touching the Mermaid Performer’s shoulder. Her voice is powerful, theatrical, and at times sinister, declaring "Go ahead and sign the scroll," singing "You poor, unfortunate soul," and pressuring the Mermaid with lines like "So how about it, cupcake?" and "Not just any ink, darling. Use mine."

Audience Silhouettes.description: Faint, dark outlines of seated individuals visible along the bottom edge of the frame, likely audience members or performers in shadow, with no discernible features due to low lighting, observing the performance and providing applause at one point.

Circular Prop.description: A large, light-colored circular object, possibly a wheel or symbolic prop, visible in the background, contributing to the mystical underwater setting.

Scroll.description: A large, beige scroll unfurled by the Mermaid Performer, displaying visible handwritten text, possibly a spell or incantation, serving as a focal point for the interaction between the performers, with the Sea Witch Performer and Flanking Performers leaning in to observe it, and its purpose confirmed by the dialogue about "signing the scroll."

raw_segments: The scene opens with the Mermaid Performer, adorned in a shimmering green mermaid tail and white seashell top, standing center stage alongside two Flanking Performers in dark, textured costumes with green and gold patterns. She holds a large gray sculpted Bust of a Man, gazing at it with a sorrowful expression as the Flanking Performers assist her, gently adjusting the green fabric draped over the bust. Throughout this, multiple female voices sing harmoniously, delivering lyrics like "No one will tell, no one will care... sweet child, dear child... will bring you to her right now... your prince and you are reconciled... together forever... Take me to her, sweet child." The stage is dimly lit with deep blue and purple Stage Lighting, creating an underwater ambiance, while the Stage Props (Rock/Coral Formations) flank the scene. A distinct, deep, guttural growl then punctuates the air. The Mermaid Performer lifts the bust, and the Flanking Performers help her carry it offstage left, leaving the stage momentarily empty as ominous, low-frequency synth music begins to build tension. The lighting dims further, and a large, ghostly projection of a man’s face appears on the backdrop, glowing faintly green. A distressed male voice calls out, "Hello? Ariel? It's me, Flounder. Where did you go?" The Mermaid Performer reappears alone, walking slowly across the stage, her movements deliberate and mournful, pausing to look up at the projection. The distressed male voice continues, "Ursula! Oh, Ariel!" as the music intensifies dramatically. She then turns to walk toward stage right, where she is joined by the Male Performer in Dark Jacket, who approaches with a pensive expression. A deeper, theatrical male voice announces, "Mistress of the deep, you have a visitor!" followed by a female voice stating, "I'm not so sure," and another female voice adding, "No, no." The Male Performer sits on a covered prop, appearing weary, while the Mermaid Performer stands beside him, still holding the bust. The scene ends with both performers in quiet contemplation under the dim, atmospheric lighting, emphasizing a moment of emotional weight and connection.

The scene opens in near darkness, bathed in deep blue stage lighting that evokes an underwater ambiance, with mist swirling around a large circular prop resembling a moon or portal. A central figure, the Mermaid Performer, identified as Ariel by the Sea Witch, stands sorrowfully clutching a large gray sculpted bust of a man, her gaze shifting between it and the approaching Sea Witch. The Sea Witch Performer, a theatrically menacing figure with spiky white hair and a voluminous dark gown, enters, addressing Ariel as "darling" and revealing herself as "Auntie Ursula." Two Flanking Performers in dark, textured costumes with green and gold patterns enter from stage right, moving with synchronized, ritualistic gestures, flanking the central figures and enhancing the scene's tension. Ursula, with a manipulative tone, dismisses Ariel's father's warnings, stating, "Nonsense, darling. We're family," and later, "We are very much alike, you and I. Gals with ambition? Nothing scares a man more, does it?" Ariel, distressed, questions her father's banishment of Ursula and confesses her love for a human, to which Ursula responds, "Oh yes. This prince fellow. Well, he's quite the catch," before proposing, "The answer is simple. You've got to become human, your..." The dramatic orchestral music underscores the tense interaction as Ariel conveys distress and resolve, while the stylized rock and coral formations reinforce the mystical underwater setting, with faint audience silhouettes observing the unfolding drama.

The scene opens with a close-up on the Sea Witch Performer, a commanding figure with spiky white hair, dramatic makeup, and a voluminous dark gown, confronting the sorrowful Mermaid Performer, who clutches a large gray sculpted bust of a man. As the camera pulls back to a wide stage view, revealing an underwater-themed set with deep blue and purple Stage Lighting, a large Circular Prop, and Stage Props resembling rock/coral formations, the Sea Witch begins to speak in a theatrical, persuasive voice, accompanied by dramatic, whimsical background music. She addresses the Mermaid, saying, "Oh, my dear, sweet child. It's what I live for. To help unfortunate merfolk like yourself," while gesturing dramatically with sweeping arms. She admits, "I admit that in the past I've been a nasty. They weren't kidding when they called me 'well, a witch'," but quickly claims, "But you'll find that nowadays, I've mended all my ways, repented, seen the light, made a switch, true?" She then boasts of her magical abilities, "Yes, and I fortunately know a little magic. It's a talent that I always have possessed," and asserts she uses it "on behalf of the miserable, lonely, and depressed." The two Flanking Performers, in matching dark, textured costumes, move slowly and ritualistically toward the center, adding to the ceremonial atmosphere as the Sea Witch continues her manipulative pitch, declaring her purpose is "For the poor, unfortunate souls, in pain, in need," offering to help those "longing to be thin" or who "wants to get the girl," with a resounding "Yes, indeed!" The Mermaid remains mostly still, her gaze fixed on the bust, as the tense, mystical confrontation unfolds amidst swirling smoke effects and focused spotlights, with faint Audience Silhouettes visible at the bottom of the frame.

The scene unfolds on a dimly lit stage, designed as an underwater grotto with deep blue and purple lighting, where the Sea Witch Performer, with spiky white hair and a voluminous dark gown, commands the center. She sings in a theatrical, manipulative voice, declaring, "Those poor, unfortunate souls, so sad, so true," as she extends her arms dramatically, seemingly casting a spell. Flanked by two performers in dark, textured costumes moving in synchronized, ritualistic motions, the Sea Witch continues, "They come flocking to my cauldron... And I help them. Yes, I do," boasting about her past "help" and sarcastic "saint" status. Opposite her, the Mermaid Performer, with long red hair and a shimmering green tail, holds a sculpted bust, displaying contemplative and sorrowful emotions that shift to defiance as she reacts to the Sea Witch's offer. The Sea Witch then directly addresses the Mermaid, offering, "So here's my best offer, babykins. I know a spell that will turn you into a human for three days." The Sea Witch explains the "procedural clause" – a kiss from the prince before the third day's sunset to stay human forever. When the Mermaid asks, "And if I don't?", the Sea Witch chillingly reveals the "small print": "Your soul!" The backdrop features stylized rock and coral formations, with a circular prop, and faint audience silhouettes are visible, all contributing to the tense, dramatic confrontation.

The scene unfolds on a dimly lit stage, bathed in deep blue and purple hues with vertical beams of light creating a mystical, underwater ambiance, underscored by a continuous, dramatic orchestral score. The Sea Witch Performer, with spiky white hair and a voluminous dark gown, commands the center stage with dramatic, sweeping gestures, her theatrical and commanding voice declaring, "is mine forever and you're doomed to spend eternity in my watch your hell soaked lair." She mockingly adds, "Lies! Don't you just love 'em?" before deliberately stating, "Of course, there is one more thing. My fee." The Mermaid Performer, with long red hair and a shimmering green mermaid tail, stands beside a large gray sculpted Bust of a Man, holding it gently with a sorrowful and contemplative expression. Her softer, more vulnerable voice responds, "But I don't have anything." The Sea Witch approaches the bust, her voice turning sinister as she reveals her demand: "I'm not asking for much. Only your voice." The Mermaid, surprised and fearful, questions, "My voice?" The Sea Witch, with a menacing reassurance, states, "Don't worry, darling. I have the perfect place for it." She then recounts, "Your grandpappy Poseidon gave his trident to your father," to which the Mermaid defensively replies, "But this was his gift pour moi." The Sea Witch reveals her knowledge of "Your magic shell," prompting a wary "You've heard about it?" from the Mermaid, who then accuses, "Father says the source of all your witchcraft." The Sea Witch dismisses this with a theatrical "Nonsense, darling!" but the Mermaid, hesitant, insists, "He says you you die without it." Feigning shock, the Sea Witch retorts, "Die? Oh, please, it's merely a bauble, a vessel for your voice." She then persuasively describes, "Trust me, darling. Your vibrato, your legato, even your belt! They'll all be safe and sound in here." The Mermaid, deeply concerned, questions, "But if I give away my voice, how can I ever..." as the two Flanking Performers in dark, textured costumes continue their synchronized, ritualistic movements, adding to the ceremonial atmosphere, all framed by gray fabric-draped Stage Props resembling rock/coral formations against a dark, textured background, intensifying the somber and pivotal mood of confrontation.

The scene unfolds on a dimly lit stage bathed in deep blue and purple hues, evoking an underwater or mystical atmosphere, underscored by a dramatic, fast-paced theatrical song. The Sea Witch Performer, with spiky white hair and a voluminous dark gown featuring a sheer skirt and black corset-like bodice, commands the left side of the stage, gesturing dramatically and moving with theatrical flair as she powerfully sings, advising on the importance of 'body language' over 'idle prattle' to attract men, declaring, 'the men up there don't like a girl who'll blabber' and 'it's much preferred for ladies not to say a word.' Opposite her, the Mermaid Performer, with long red hair and a shimmering green mermaid tail, holds a large gray sculpted bust of a man, her expression shifting between sorrow and contemplation as she listens to the Sea Witch's persuasive, slightly sinister lyrics. The Sea Witch continues, emphasizing that 'she who holds her tongue gets her man,' before directly offering a deal to the Mermaid, urging her to 'Make your choice!' and stating, 'It won't cost much, just your voice,' warning, 'If you want to cross the bridge, my sweet, you gotta pay the toll.' Flanking the Mermaid are two performers in matching dark, textured costumes with green and gold patterns, who move slowly and synchronously toward the center, adding a ritualistic tone to the unfolding confrontation. The moody lighting, with spotlights highlighting the central figures and faint Audience Silhouettes visible, enhances the dramatic and symbolic atmosphere of this pivotal moment centered around the bust and the Sea Witch's compelling, manipulative offer.

The scene opens with a close-up of the Sea Witch Performer, adorned in a voluminous dark gown with purple embellishments and spiky white hair, holding a glowing cauldron, while the Mermaid Performer, in a shimmering green tail and white seashell top, unfurls a large scroll with visible handwritten text. The stage is bathed in deep purple and blue lighting, with a mystical circular backdrop resembling an underwater portal. The Sea Witch Performer's commanding voice declares, "Go ahead and sign the scroll. Got some jets and now I got a voice. The boss is on our own," followed by a dramatic, drawn-out rendition of "You poor, unfortunate soul," accompanied by building, theatrical music. As the camera pulls back, the full stage reveals two large, curved rock/coral formations flanking the central area, and the Mermaid Performer is joined by two Flanking Performers in dark, textured green-and-gold costumes. The trio gathers around the scroll, with the Sea Witch Performer gesturing dramatically and the Flanking Performers leaning in with intense expressions. After a moment of audience applause, the Sea Witch Performer, in a more conversational yet still commanding tone, asks, "So how about it, cupcake?" The Mermaid Performer appears to be reading or reciting from the scroll, her expression shifting from focused to distressed as she pleads, "But if I become human, I'll never see my family again. My sisters, even my father." The Sea Witch Performer then presses her, "Now, do you want Princey or not?" and counts down urgently, "Five, four, three, two!" The Mermaid Performer, desperate and resigned, exclaims, "Yes! Yes, I'll do it! Where do I sign?" The Sea Witch Performer then reaches out, touching the Mermaid Performer’s shoulder, and they share a moment of intense interaction, possibly a confrontation or revelation, as the Sea Witch Performer sinisterly instructs, "Not just any ink, darling. Use mine." A magical "whoosh" sound and a gasp from the Mermaid Performer follow, while the Flanking Performers react with exaggerated, theatrical gestures, adding to the ritualistic atmosphere. The lighting remains moody and dynamic, with beams of purple and blue spotlighting the central group. The audience silhouettes are visible at the bottom of the frame, observing the unfolding drama. The scene culminates in a moment of physical contact between the Sea Witch Performer and the Mermaid Performer, as the Flanking Performers step back slightly, suggesting a pivotal moment in the narrative, all set to dramatic, concluding music. The scroll remains a central prop, its text partially legible, hinting at a spell or incantation. The overall atmosphere is dark, mystical, and emotionally charged, emphasizing themes of magic, power, and transformation.

event_blocks.summary: On a dark, atmospheric stage illuminated by deep blue and purple Stage Lighting, evoking an underwater realm with faint Audience Silhouettes in the foreground, a large, circular, translucent prop stands center, flanked by curved, horn-like Stage Props resembling coral. The Sea Witch Performer, adorned in a voluminous dark gown with spiky white hair and dramatic makeup, holds a glowing cauldron and delivers a powerful, operatic, and menacing incantation: "Beluga, Sevruga, come winds of the Caspian Sea... Laryngitis, love laryngitis, love fortune of me." She then addresses the Mermaid Performer, who has long red hair, a shimmering green tail, and holds a large Scroll with handwritten text, her expression distressed. The Sea Witch Performer commands, "Now sing! Sing your voice over to me!" The Mermaid Performer responds with a long, melancholic, wordless vocalization, a sorrowful wail. Two Flanking Performers in matching dark, textured costumes enter from stage right, moving slowly toward the center with synchronized, theatrical gestures to observe the interaction. The Sea Witch Performer urges the Mermaid to "Sing, come, sing, sing!" before releasing a triumphant cackle and commanding, "Now swim! Swim, swim, swim for your life! Heal, my child!" as her laughter grows maniacal. The Sea Witch Performer departs, leaving the Mermaid Performer alone with the scroll. The lighting dims, the stage fills with mist, and the Mermaid Performer's sorrowful vocalizations continue, underscored by the fading maniacal laughter of the Sea Witch and a sustained, low, ominous musical note, as the scene fades to near-blackness, highlighting the Mermaid Performer’s solitude amidst the faintly visible Stage Props and Circular Prop.

A large, circular, translucent prop resembling a bubble or portal dominates the center, flanked by two large, curved, horn-like structures resembling coral. The Stage Lighting casts dynamic beams that shift in intensity and color, enhancing the mystical ambiance. The Sea Witch Performer, with spiky white hair, dramatic makeup, and a voluminous dark gown, commands the stage, holding a glowing cauldron as she sings an operatic, powerful, and slightly menacing incantation: "Beluga, Sevruga, come winds of the Caspian Sea... Laryngitis, love laryngitis, love fortune of me." She then turns to the Mermaid Performer, who has long red hair and a shimmering green tail, holding a large Scroll with visible handwritten text, her expression distressed. The Sea Witch Performer touches the Mermaid's shoulder and commands, "Now sing! Sing your voice over to me!" In response, the Mermaid Performer begins a long, melancholic, wordless vocalization, a sorrowful wail, as two Flanking Performers in matching dark, textured costumes enter from stage right, moving slowly toward the center with synchronized, theatrical gestures, leaning in to observe the scroll and reacting to the interaction. event_blocks.summary: The scene begins in a dimly lit, underwater-themed setting with blue and green stage lighting, where the Mermaid Performer, distressed and holding a scroll, is comforted by the kneeling Seagull Performer. The Yellow-Striped Performer declares the mermaid has traded her voice for legs, prompting the Seagull Performer's lament and a comment from a slumped Male Performer in Dark Jacket near a Bust of a Man. The Yellow-Striped Performer explains the three-day deadline to win the prince's love with a kiss. As the camera widens, revealing a Banner with Emblem and Ensemble Performers with Translucent Umbrellas on a ship-like Stage Platform, upbeat music starts. The Yellow-Striped Performer leads the ensemble in dance, while the Seagull Performer encourages the Mermaid Performer. The Red-Haired Performer sings about positivity as the ensemble dances on the ship-like platform, with the King Triton Performer standing prominently. The Red-Haired Performer interacts with the Mermaid Performer, who unfurls the scroll, while the Seagull Performer comforts her. The Male Performer in Dark Jacket (lying) remains slumped, and the Male Performer in Dark Jacket (standing) sits pensively. The Red-Haired Performer exits, leaving the Mermaid Performer with the bust. The ensemble continues their choreography as the King Triton Performer raises his trident. The Red-Haired Performer questions the word "positivity," then affirms its power. The scene transitions to a cavernous underwater stage with dynamic lighting, where the ensemble, dressed as seagulls, performs a synchronized dance on the ship-like platform, accompanied by squawking sounds. The Red-Haired Performer gestures expressively, narrating the performance. The Mermaid Performer is lifted onto the ensemble's shoulders, and a Seagull Performer leans over her. The music shifts, and the Red-Haired Performer sings about positivity, encouraging action. The ensemble, in seagull costumes, dances on the ship-like platform, emitting squawking sounds, while the Mermaid Performer interacts with them and is lifted in a celebratory formation. The Red-Haired Performer gestures expressively, and the scene is set against a rocky coastline backdrop.

Stage Props (Rock/Coral Formations).description: Two large, covered props resembling rocks or coral formations, draped in gray fabric, positioned on stage, serving as part of the scenic design and contributing to the underwater environment, flanking the central performance area.

Mermaid Performer.description: A performer with long red hair, wearing a shimmering green mermaid tail and a white top with seashell embellishments, holding a large scroll with visible handwritten text, displaying focused and distressed expressions as she interacts with the Seagull Performer and others.

Red-Haired Performer.description: A performer in a full-body red lobster costume with a top hat and large googly eyes, who stands to the right of the ensemble, gesturing expressively as if narrating or reacting to the performance, remaining stationary while the ensemble dances, and is heard singing a show tune about 'positivity' from 0:49 onwards.

Yellow-Striped Performer.description: A performer in a bright yellow and blue striped costume with spiky yellow hair, resembling a stylized sea creature, who leads the ensemble with animated gestures and expressive movements, often stepping forward to command attention, vocally explaining the Mermaid's situation ('legs, you idiot. She traded her voice to the Sea Witch and got her legs,' 'not a sound'), dramatically questioning her father's reaction, outlining her goal ('Make the prince fall in love with her'), and disagreeing with advice ('She'll do no such thing').

Seagull Performer.description: A performer in a large, comical seagull costume with a yellow beak, white feathery wings, and a gray body, leaning over the Mermaid Performer, placing a hand on her shoulder in a comforting or theatrical manner, then standing up to gesture toward the ensemble, and is part of the group making the 'squawking' and 'cawing' sounds.

Audience Silhouettes.description: Faint, dark outlines of seated individuals visible along the bottom edge of the frame, likely audience members or performers in shadow, with no discernible features due to low lighting, observing the performance.

Stage Lighting.description: Vertical streaks of deep blue and faint green light projected onto a dark backdrop, creating a textured, atmospheric effect that spotlights the central performers and enhances the underwater illusion, with dynamic beams shifting to emphasize key moments.

Translucent Umbrellas.description: Pastel-colored, semi-transparent umbrellas carried by ensemble performers, adding a whimsical, ethereal quality to the choreography and stage presence.

Scroll.description: A large, beige scroll unfurled by the Mermaid Performer, displaying visible handwritten text, possibly a spell or incantation, serving as a focal point for the interaction between the performers, with the Sea Witch Performer and Flanking Performers leaning in to observe it.

Bust of a Man.description: A large, gray, sculpted bust of a man with detailed facial features, held by the Mermaid Performer, serving as a focal point for the scene’s emotional and symbolic weight.

Ensemble Performers.description: A group of performers on a raised platform resembling a ship’s deck, dressed in colorful, period-style costumes resembling seagulls with ruffled sleeves and skirts, performing a choreographed dance or musical number under blue stage lighting, and vocally contributing loud, rhythmic 'squawking' and 'cawing' sounds, mimicking seagulls, throughout the first 49 seconds of the segment.

Stage Platform.description: A raised wooden platform resembling a ship’s deck, with railings and lanterns, serving as the main performance area for the ensemble, where the choreographed dance takes place.

Banner with Emblem.description: A large, beige banner hanging behind the ensemble, displaying a stylized emblem resembling a cross or anchor, set against a backdrop depicting a stormy sea or rocky coastline.

Male Performer in Dark Jacket (lying).description: A male performer in a white ruffled shirt, blue pants, and black boots, initially lying slumped against the bust, appearing unconscious or resting, with his head tilted back and eyes closed; later, he is helped to sit up and gradually regains consciousness, his eyes fluttering open.

Male Performer in Dark Jacket (standing).description: A male performer with short dark hair, wearing a white ruffled shirt, blue knee-length breeches, a red sash, and black boots, who walks slowly across the stage with a pensive expression, eventually sitting on a covered prop, appearing weary or reflective.

King Triton Performer.description: A central male performer with long silver hair, a golden crown, and a bare chest, wearing a blue and gold lower garment, holding a golden trident, engaging in dramatic gestures and dialogue with the Mermaid Performer, later raising his trident in a commanding pose.

raw_segments: The scene opens in near darkness, illuminated by vertical streaks of deep blue and faint green stage lighting that create an atmospheric, underwater illusion, accompanied by a whimsical yet melancholy orchestral score. As the lights gradually intensify, a spotlight reveals the Mermaid Performer, with long red hair and a shimmering green tail, kneeling on stage and clutching a large scroll, her body language conveying sorrow or desperation. Suddenly, the Seagull Performer enters from stage left, leaning over her with a comforting gesture, placing a hand on her shoulder. His comical costume features a large yellow beak, white feathery wings, and a gray body. He then speaks in a male voice, observing, "Well, look who got beached." He continues with a playful, inquisitive tone, "Wait, there's something different about you. Nope, nope. Don't tell me. I'll decipher it for myself," making thoughtful "Hmm" sounds before exclaiming, "Aha! It's your hairdo. You've been using the Dinglehopper." Shortly after, the Red-Haired Performer in a red lobster costume with a top hat and googly eyes enters from stage right, gesturing expressively, and the Yellow-Striped Performer, dressed in a bright yellow and blue striped outfit with spiky yellow hair, joins from stage left, both engaging with the Mermaid Performer. The three performers form a small group around her, with the Seagull Performer remaining close, while the Red-Haired and Yellow-Striped Performers stand slightly behind, observing. The backdrop depicts a stylized underwater landscape with rock formations, and two large covered props resembling coral or rocks flank the stage. The lighting shifts to include soft white beams from above, enhancing the dramatic mood. The Mermaid Performer remains the emotional focal point, her interaction with the Seagull Performer suggesting a moment of solace or revelation, while the others watch with concern. As the Seagull finishes his deduction, a female voice, likely the Mermaid's, begins to respond, "She's got...", before the segment ends. Audience silhouettes remain visible along the bottom edge, observing the unfolding drama.

The scene opens with a close-up of four performers on a dark, underwater-themed stage, lit by blue and green hues and flanked by rock/coral formations. The distressed Mermaid Performer, seated and holding a scroll, is comforted by the kneeling Seagull Performer, who places a hand on her shoulder. The Yellow-Striped Performer animatedly declares, "legs, you idiot. She traded her voice to the Sea Witch and got her legs," while the Seagull Performer laments, "No. Not your beautiful pipes." The Yellow-Striped Performer confirms, "Yes, see, not a sound." The Red-Haired Performer in a lobster costume gestures expressively before exiting stage right. A Male Performer in a Dark Jacket, slumped against a Bust of a Man, adds, "Ah, kid, it hurts me to say, but that, that was a lousy swap." The Yellow-Striped Performer dramatically questions, "What would her father say? I'll tell you what her father would say, her father would say he'd kill himself a crab, that's what he'd say." A female voice reveals, "She's got just three days," prompting the Male Performer to ask, "Three days to what?" The Yellow-Striped Performer explains, "Make the prince fall in love with her," and the female voice adds, "And to prove it, he gets to... I mean, he's got to... kiss her." The Male Performer advises, "Well, if you want to snare the prince, you got to learn a graceful way of perambulating," but the Yellow-Striped Performer retorts, "She'll do no such thing." The Male Performer encourages, "Sure she will. Come on now, upsy-daisy." As the camera pulls back to a wider stage view, revealing a Banner with Emblem and Ensemble Performers in period costumes with translucent umbrellas emerging from behind the rock formations onto a ship's deck-like Stage Platform, upbeat, theatrical music begins. The Yellow-Striped Performer leads the group in choreographed movement, while the Seagull Performer stands and gestures toward the ensemble, then encourages, "What's this? You giving up so soon? That's not like you. You gotta have a little gumption, a can-do kind of attitude. Take it from a gull who knows." The Red-Haired Performer briefly re-enters before exiting again. The ensemble engages in a synchronized dance as the lighting intensifies, creating a dramatic atmosphere, with the Mermaid Performer remaining isolated, emphasizing her emotional state amidst the performance.

A group of Ensemble Performers, dressed in colorful, ruffled period costumes with yellow hats and holding Translucent Umbrellas, perform a synchronized, lively dance on a raised Stage Platform resembling a ship’s deck. The music then shifts to a more rhythmic, percussive section with a chorus joining in, singing, "You can flip and you clap 'til your wish comes true!" event_blocks.summary: In near darkness, faint blue and green stage lighting gradually illuminated a textured backdrop resembling an underwater cavern, accompanied by dramatic, slightly ominous orchestral music and subtle underwater sound effects. Two large, draped props resembling coral or rock formations flanked the central stage area. The King Triton Performer, shirtless with long silver hair, a golden crown, and a blue-and-gold lower garment, stood center stage holding a golden trident. He was joined by two Mermaid Performers from the ensemble, one with blue hair and a shimmering blue-and-gold tail, the other with pink hair and a pink-and-gold tail, both adorned with seashell headdresses and flowing, translucent veils. The trio engaged in a slow-motion interaction, with the Mermaid Performers gesturing toward each other and King Triton. The blue-haired mermaid called out, "Your Majesty!" King Triton asked, "Have you found her?" The pink-haired mermaid replied, "We searched everywhere!" The blue-haired mermaid added, "We found no trace of your daughter." King Triton commanded, "Well, keep looking until you do!" to which the pink-haired mermaid responded, "Yes, sire." The blue-haired mermaid stated, "I never meant to wish her ill, Daddy," prompting King Triton to reply, "I know, I know." She then expressed concern about singing her sister's solos if she never returned. The pink-haired mermaid interjected, "Don't call us," before declaring, "We won't rest. Not until she's home." King Triton, with a weary and melancholic tone, thanked them, adding, "As if the loss of your mother wasn't enough," as cool, atmospheric lighting continued to enhance the underwater illusion, with Audience Silhouettes observing in shadow, while the stage remained uncluttered, focusing attention on the central figures and their symbolic gestures, underscored by the ongoing music.
